polls.text:
Do you agree we should adopt the two IETF Chair documents (https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-eggert-ietf-chair-may-delegate/ 
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-eggert-ietf-chair-standin/ as WG items

chat.author: Eric Rescorla
chat.text: I would argue that this kind of editorial stuff is implicitly in scope of any WG

chat.author: C. Heard
chat.text: Not an active WG member, but I strongly support the proposed charter revision. These documents affect everyone who participates in the IETF, and documenting actual  policy is important.

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: +1 To Pete

chat.author: Eric Rescorla
chat.text: I'm actually going to create an ISEG and make a bunch of decisions

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: "...removing tool dependencies that have changed since 2026/2418"?

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: "...editorial changes that do not affect the scope of the document are allowed"?

chat.author: C. Heard
chat.text: +1 to "...removing tool dependencies that have changed since 2026/2418".

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: probably better "document the existing  processes and practices".    I'm particularly concerned about IESG Statements...   Hmm, starting writing this earlier, but I think I'm supporting what Lars is saying.

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: Does trimming the rest give the chairs/AD enough to push back on certain controversial proposals?

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: collecting the bunch of documents was a viable approach when we started this group, now that we're close to finishing the docs, I don't think it's an appropriate activitiy.

chat.author: Eric Rescorla
chat.text: It seems to me that just listing those two might imply that other inputs were not permitted?

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: Charters exist to (among other things) constrain WGs so that the Evil Guys can't hijack it to do Bad Things. While this wordsmithing is kind of reasonable, I think the current WG state is that it's unlikely to do Bad Things no matter which of the suggested variants we adopt.

chat.author: Rich Salz
chat.text: If everyone else believes the Charter protects us (or at least forces attackers to go up the chain), then I am fine being in the rough.

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: My take is that it's more important that this discussion finishes than the exact wording adopted, given the range of alternatives suggested.

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: I know it's a PITA, but a complete list of changes (probably produceable by handing the diff to some AI thingy) would be a good way to assure we know we have consensus on the changes. (It doesn't need to remain in the published RFC.)

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: (I am anal retentive enough to go through the diff every time. Others are not so committed.)

chat.author: Jari Arkko
chat.text: Pete: we have a complete list of current changes, look for email from me on 2026 bis. I can send another one on 2418.

chat.author: Jari Arkko
chat.text: Current changes + why those changes were made (RFC ref/editorial/etc)

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: Somehow I missed that message. Peachy.

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Just looked at the current "Unicode" comment in 9.1.1 and I'm still feeling anxious.   The problem is not only that "Unicode" is a moving target, sometimes with substantive and , occasionally incompatible, changes between versions, but there is ambiguity as to whether "UTS" documents are part of "the standard" or not.   "Unicode encoding scheme" is actually correct, but the citation is much too broad for it.    Even if you don't use "ASCII", it would be better to pick something more stable and, more important, something in which the reference does not require qualification or explanation.

chat.author: Eric Rescorla
chat.text: This text does in fact seem to say you could create WG Realtors

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: If you are going to go into this much detail, it seems to me that it is important to clarify whether those decisions can be appealed.

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Also not clear that ADs can appoint and dismiss those WG Chairs

chat.author: Jari Arkko
chat.text: John: Take your point. Not as a good of a reference example then, on basis of statbility. Perhaps find another example (or keep current)?

chat.author: Lars Eggert
chat.text: I think this is the same question I asked - are there foundational docs for these roles

chat.author: Rich Salz
chat.text: Okay, what is the right link for Unicode encoding scheme?

chat.author: Rich Salz
chat.text: @John Klensin  ^^ that's for you.  email answer is fine.

chat.author: Jean Mahoney
chat.text: https://authors.ietf.org/en/reference-style-guidance#unicode-consortium

chat.author: Jari Arkko
chat.text: Lars: There are no foundational documents for these roles (AFAIK)

chat.author: Jari Arkko
chat.text: And if that's the case, what is your recommendation? Remove undefined things? Define those things?

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Jari and Rich: my preference would be to pick another example entirely (or stick with ASCII).  A different example should find something much more stable and self-contained. such as the IEEE 802 Ethernet definition.  Rich, the problem with "Unicode encoding standard" is that it would require the reference to point to a specific section of a particular standard.  While we do that  sometimes in practice, I thing would be better kept it out of this document.

chat.author: Eric Rescorla
chat.text: I have to step out. Lars, I haven't had enough time to really review your revised delegate drafts, but my overall opinions on what should be delegatable haven't changed.

chat.author: Rich Salz
chat.text: @John Klensin okay, thanks.

chat.author: Jari Arkko
chat.text: Ethernet reference or similar would be good example

chat.author: Rich Salz
chat.text: Ok, I will go with etherernet not unicode.

chat.author: Lars Eggert
chat.text: Idle thought: Do we have any existing doc that says something like "IETF participants are humans"?

chat.author: C. Heard
chat.text: Please do not leave inaccurate text in.

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: I am now imagining Harald sleeping in that tiny little bunk bed behind him.

chat.author: C. Heard
chat.text: And defend the change against the objectors.

chat.author: Jari Arkko
chat.text: @lars: must be humans or, if not, all responsibility must still rest on a human?

chat.author: C. Heard
chat.text: +1 to Bron.

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: +1 also to Bron

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: +1 to removal

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: we could hum

chat.author: Roman Danyliw
chat.text: "An adoption call for consensus is not consensus on the content"

chat.author: Roman Danyliw
chat.text: "An adoption call is not consensus on the content"

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: "Adopting a document does not imply the WG has consensus on the content of the document or committed to eventually publishing it."

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: (It is not clear to me that adoption of a document requires WG consensus.)

chat.author: Lars Eggert
chat.text: For the last sentence, I said to replace "consensus" with "outcomes of the deliberations".

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: @Pete it's my understanding that wg consensus is not required to adopt something

chat.author: Ted Lemon
chat.text: Can the stand-in designate a different stand-in?

chat.author: Eliot Lear
chat.text: "Guests of guests may not have guests"

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: If the goal is to solve the "more than a full time position" problem, then the community should be reviewing the IETF Chair role and splitting it up, not trying to create delegation rules and refining them.

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: Are we discussing contents or are we discussing adoption?

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: (Because arguing about the meaning of Latin terms is always useful...)

chat.author: Jay Daley
chat.text: I am not showing a hand!

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: does the slide clicker get to answer the poll?

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Show of hands does not seem to be working in Meetecho

chat.author: Pete Resnick
chat.text: Worked in the lite tool.

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: And the full tool

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Sorry... didn't see that part of the screen

chat.author: Sean Turner
chat.text: :man_shrugging:
